name: Peñas del Viento via Argatón Loop from La Arena
name_original: Peña de los Vientos por Argatón – circular desde La Arena
description: Tackle the challenging Peña de los Vientos por Argatón circular road cycling route from La Arena, featuring steep climbs and panoramic coast
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Soto Del Barco are characterized by their varied terrain, encompassing river valleys, coastal roads, and rolling hills. The region features routes that follow the Nalón River, offering flatter sections, alongside paths that ascend into the surrounding elevated areas. This landscape provides a mix of gradients suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Soto Del Barco The most popular road cycling route is Peñas del Viento via Argatón Loop from La Arena, a 39.7 miles (63.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bridge Over the Nalón River – Playa de los Quebrantos loop from San Esteban de Pravia, a moderate 45.5 miles (73.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Nalón River and coastal sections near Playa de los Quebrantos. Local road cyclists also love the Bridge over the Nalón River – Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail leading through the Nalón River valley and towards Muros de Nalón, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Road cycling around Soto Del Barco is defined by river valleys, coastal roads, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy road bike trails, moderate distances, and advanced road cycling routes. The routes in Soto Del Barco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 5,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Soto Del Barco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Soto Del Barco?
A: Soto Del Barco offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 90 options available for exploration, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Soto Del Barco?
A: The routes in Soto Del Barco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, coastal roads, and rolling hills, offering diverse challenges and scenic views.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Soto Del Barco?
A: Yes, Soto Del Barco features several easier road cycling routes. The region's landscape includes flatter sections along the Nalón River, which are ideal for those new to road cycling or looking for a less strenuous ride. There are 9 routes specifically categorized as easy.

Q: Where can I find advanced road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Soto Del Barco offers 37 difficult routes with significant elevation gain. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069305118/penas-del-viento-via-argaton-loop-from-la-arena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas del Viento via Argatón Loop from La Arena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 39.7 miles (63.9 km) and features over 1,490 meters of ascent through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations for road cycling can vary, routes that follow the Nalón River often present flatter, more manageable sections suitable for families. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances to ensure a pleasant experience for all.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Soto Del Barco?
A: Road cycling routes around Soto Del Barco are characterized by their varied terrain, encompassing river valleys, coastal roads, and rolling hills. You'll find routes that follow the Nalón River, offering flatter sections, alongside paths that ascend into the surrounding elevated areas, providing a mix of gradients.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in Soto Del Barco?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Soto Del Barco are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40388308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Nalón River – Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you might encounter historical sites and natural beauty. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt;, or the architectural marvel of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2005855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer interesting stops along your ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornellana Recreation Area – San Martín de Lodón Church loop from Soto del Barco/Sotu'l Barcu&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans 60.5 miles (97.4 km) and includes over 1,400 meters of elevation gain, making it suitable for endurance training.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Soto Del Barco?
A: The region's coastal influence generally provides mild conditions. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded roads, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winter rides are possible but may require checking local weather conditions for rain or cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate coastal sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Nalón River – Playa de los Quebrantos loop from San Esteban de Pravia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.5 miles (73.3 km) path that offers views of the Nalón River and coastal sections near Playa de los Quebrantos.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through Muros de Nalón?
A: Absolutely. Muros de Nalón is a common point of interest for road cyclists in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31922469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón – Xilo Beach loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.1 miles (34 km) route that explores this charming coastal town and its surroundings.
tips: The Nalón river becomes at its mouth between San Esteban de Pravia and San Juan de la Arena in the Pravia estuary. The Nalón is 153 kilometers long and rises in the port of Tarna, in the mountains of Caso.

Or Peña Cesteiro is located at 723 meters above sea level and is a well-known peak in the province of Asturias for road bike lovers. It has two sides; la Rondiella, with a drop of 643m, a length of 7.3 km, an average slope of 8.80 and a maximum slope of 20%. On this side the road is in quite bad condition.
The other slope, in better condition, is known as Argatón. With a drop of 626m, a length of 6.88 km, an average slope of 9.10% and a maximum of 17%.

Between Las Dueñas and Muros de Nalón we come across this beautiful 1,184 meter long viaduct that crosses the valleys formed by the Uncín and Especuero rivers. Shell-shaped, this viaduct favors cyclists thanks to the reduction in traffic on this road.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Peña de los Vientos por Argatón circular route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, particularly known for its challenging climb to Peña de los Vientos. The ascent from Argatón features steep ramps, with sections reaching up to 20% gradient and an average gradient exceeding 9% over 6.9 kilometers. It requires very good fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route from La Arena?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for a circular route starting from La Arena are not provided, La Arena is a common starting point for routes in the area. It is advisable to look for public parking areas within La Arena village, keeping in mind that the roads on the climb itself are very narrow and not suitable for parking.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: As you ascend past Cipiello, the route opens up to splendid panoramic views of the Cantabrian coast. The summit area of Peña de los Vientos (Sierra de los Vientos) offers immeasurable vistas, and you'll likely see wind turbines towards the top. The route is largely secluded, providing an immersive natural landscape experience.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you might encounter notable features such as the Bridge over the Nalón River and the Concha de Artedo Viaduct. The route also passes near Peña Cesteiro, a summit offering great views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for strong winds at higher elevations, the best time to cycle this route would generally be during the milder seasons, such as late spring, summer, and early autumn. This avoids the harsher conditions of winter and ensures better visibility for the panoramic views.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is not suitable for beginner road cyclists. The climb to Peña de los Vientos features sustained steep gradients, narrow roads, and can be very demanding, requiring a high level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, typically, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle on public roads in this region of Asturias, Spain. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, there are generally no specific restrictions against dogs. However, for their safety and yours, dogs should not accompany cyclists on such challenging and potentially busy routes, especially given the narrow roads and steep gradients.

faqs.question: What is the recommended descent for this route?
faqs.answer: For the descent, it is highly recommended to return via Argatón. This road is generally wider and in better condition than other options, while still offering spectacular views. The ascent road from Argatón is very narrow and can be irregular, making it less ideal for a fast or technical descent.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The climb itself is largely devoid of inhabited houses and amenities. You'll find few signs of habitation, with only a few old cattle sheds. It's crucial to carry all necessary water and nutrition with you, as there are no cafes or dedicated water stops directly on the challenging ascent to Peña de los Vientos.

faqs.question: How can I navigate this route, especially given the narrow roads and lack of signage?
faqs.answer: Given the narrow roads and reported lack of clear signage, it is highly recommended to use a reliable GPS device or a cycling app like komoot with the route downloaded offline. This will help you stay on track and navigate confidently, especially on the more remote sections of the climb.
